sections.paragraph:
to break, only in Kal in the phrase פָּרַס לֶחֶם לְ to break one’s bread to any one; i.e. to distribute it, Isa 58:7 and without לֶחֶם Jerem. 16:7 . Compare פָּרַשׁ No. 1.

Hiphil-

(1) to cleave, to divide. Levit. 11:4 , וּפַרְסָה אֵינֶנּוּ מַפְרִיס “but cleaveth not the hoof,” i.e. has not the hoof altogether cloven. Elsewhere-

(2) הִפְרִיס פַּרְסָה Levit. 11:3 , Lev 11:6, Lev 11:7, 26 Lev 11:26 Deu 14:7, Deu 14:8and without פַּרְסָה is nothing more than to have (pr. to make or produce) a cloven hoof.

Derivatives, פֶּרֶס, פַּרְסָה.

פרס

in pause פָּרָ֑ס pr.n. Heb. and Ch. Persia, the Persians, 2Ch 36:20, 22 2Ch 36:22 Ezr 1:1 4:5 , seqq.; 6:14 Dan 5:28 6:9 , 13 . Pers. يارس, نارس, فرس. Hence Gent. noun פַּרְסִי a Persian, Nehem. 12:22 and Ch. emphat. פַּרְסָיָא Dan. 6:29 .
